title: Lithocarpus rajah
taxonomy:
Lithocarpus rajah belongs to the family Fagaceae. 
It is a species of evergreen tree native to Borneo. 
Lithocarpus rajah is classified under the genus Lithocarpus and the order Fagales. 
The species is also known as the Rajah oak tree. 
It is closely related to other species within the Lithocarpus genus.
uses: Lithocarpus rajah, a species of evergreen tree native to Borneo, has been found to possess medicinal properties. The bark and leaves of the tree are traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever and respiratory issues. The extracts of Lithocarpus rajah have been studied for their potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Additionally, the tree's extracts have been shown to exhibit antioxidant activity, which may contribute to its potential health benefits. intro: Lithocarpus rajah, also known as Rajah tree or Rajah Lithocarpus, is a species of evergreen tree in the beech family (Fagaceae). The plant is prized for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it beneficial for the treatment of various health conditions such as fever, cough, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Lithocarpus rajah include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Lithocarpus rajah is commonly prepared as a decoction, tea, or powder to treat a range of health issues, and is also used in herbal remedies for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects.
